Practice Management

Free practice management software for therapists: do they exist?

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

Free practice management software for therapists: do they exist?

You started your practice to see clients, not to hunt for software. Yet here you are, juggling spreadsheets, sticky notes, and a calendar that keeps double-booking. You’ve heard rumours of “free EHRs” and wonder if one could buy you time before cash flow steadies. This post walks you through what’s genuinely free, what’s just a free trial, and whether the cheapest paid plans from TheraNest or SimplePractice are worth the leap.

Quick verdict: 100 % free EHRs exist, but they cap clients, skip insurance billing, or need tech skills. If you bill insurance or want telehealth, budget at least US $39/mo for TheraNest or SimplePractice.

What you actually get with free practice management software

Free tools fall into three buckets:

  1. Student or charity licences – SimplePractice Student Plan (5 clients max), WriteUpp UK charity (≤10 clients/mo).
  2. Open-source – OpenEMR, OscarEMR. You host them yourself; no vendor support.
  3. Discontinued grandfathered plans – TheraNest Legacy Free ended in 2019; new users can’t join.

All free tiers share common limits: no ERA/EFT insurance, no telehealth, capped clients, and you handle your own HIPAA/GDPR compliance if you self-host.

TheraNest – entry plan breakdown

Pricing
US $39, UK £39, AU $59, CA $49 per month. 21-day free trial, no card required.

Core features

  • Unlimited clients and notes
  • Scheduling with email reminders
  • ICD-10 codes and ERA/EFT for US/CA
  • Basic telehealth add-on (+US $10/mo)
  • Free CSV import/export

Pros

  • Cheapest plan already includes insurance tools
  • No client limit
  • Australian and Canadian dollar pricing available

Cons

  • Telehealth costs extra
  • UK/AU users must use Stripe; no integrated clearinghouse
  • No free tier after trial

Best for
Solo US or Canadian therapists who bill insurance and want electronic remittance without upgrading to a higher plan.

Start your 21-day TheraNest trial here Try THERANEST free →

SimplePractice – entry plan breakdown

Pricing
US $39, UK £30, AU $55, CA $45 per month. 30-day free trial; card required but not charged.

Core features

  • Unlimited clients and notes
  • Telehealth included (1:1 sessions)
  • Client portal for booking/forms
  • Autopay via Stripe
  • Free import from 20+ platforms

Pros

  • Telehealth and portal included—no add-ons
  • Phone support in the US
  • SOC-2 compliance on top of HIPAA/GDPR/PIPEDA

Cons

  • Group telehealth costs extra
  • UK price looks lower, but NHS codes not supported
  • No perpetual free tier

Best for
Solo therapists who want telehealth and a client portal straight out of the box without extra plug-ins.

Grab your 30-day SimplePractice trial here Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →

Head-to-head: TheraNest vs SimplePractice cheapest plan

FeatureTheraNest StarterSimplePractice Starter
Monthly cost (US)US $39US $39
Free trial length21 days, no card30 days, card
TelehealthUS $10 add-onIncluded (1:1)
Insurance ERAIncludedIncluded
Client portalNoYes
StorageUnlimited fair-useUnlimited fair-use
UK pricing£39£30
SupportEmail/chatEmail/chat + phone (US)

Who should choose which

Pick TheraNest if you bill US or Canadian insurance and want ERA/EFT without paying for extras. The workflow is built for claims first, telehealth second.

Pick SimplePractice if you run most sessions online or want clients to book and pay through a portal. The telehealth quota is generous for 1:1 work and the client portal cuts admin e-mails.

If you’re cash-strapped with under 10 clients and no insurance, apply for WriteUpp’s UK charity licence or use SimplePractice Student Plan—just expect to migrate later when you grow.

FAQ

Is there a completely free therapy EHR that supports insurance?

No. Every free option skips ERA/EFT. You’ll still log into payer portals to download remittance, which eats more time than the US $39 you’d save.

What features are missing in free counselling practice management software UK versions?

Free UK licences (WriteUpp charity, open-source) lack NHS Read codes and automated GDPR data-retention rules. You must set those manually.

Are free platforms HIPAA / GDPR / PIPEDA compliant?

They can be, but open-source puts the compliance burden on you. Hosted free tiers (student plans) are compliant, yet cap clients.

When should I upgrade from a free plan?

Upgrade when you hit the client limit, need telehealth, or spend more than an hour a week on manual billing. At US $39, one reclaimed hour pays for itself.

Can I import my free-plan data into SimplePractice later?

Yes. SimplePractice imports CSV exports from most free tools, including TheraNest, WriteUpp, and OpenEMR, at no cost.

Bottom line

Free practice management software for therapists exists, but the limits hit fast—usually at 5–10 clients and zero insurance tools. If you’re past the startup phase, the lowest-cost paid plans save more time than they cost. Start the 30-day SimplePractice trial here Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free → and import your client list free; if it’s not for you, export everything and walk away—no charge, no risk.
